fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Street Frontage
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness on the property could include maintaining clear and accessible multiple access points, which may facilitate quicker entry for first responders. Adequate signage and lighting at these access points could further aid in navigation during an emergency situation. Managing the existing dense vegetation, particularly around the building perimeter and access routes, may improve visibility and movement for emergency personnel. Ensuring that pedestrian walkways are unobstructed and clearly defined can facilitate safe and efficient movement throughout the site. Keeping the vehicle parking area organized and free from encroachments may support effective staging of emergency vehicles. Collectively, these measures could assist in streamlining emergency operations and improving overall responder safety and efficiency.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Tree Cluster; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetated Perimeter
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between vegetation and the structure by trimming back or removing vegetation directly adjacent to the building could improve defensible space and reduce the potential for fire exposure. Thinning out and potentially removing some of the trees within the cluster near the property can further support fuel continuity reduction and improve the defensible space. Managing the vegetated perimeter by reducing the density and continuity of the vegetation can create additional breaks that may help slow fire movement. Maintaining clear and accessible routes for firefighter movement around the property, especially in light of the existing vegetation, would support suppression efforts. Proactive long-term vegetation management practices can contribute to improved wildfire resilience and preparedness for the property by maintaining adequate defensible space and managing fuel loads along and within the perimeter.
